description_long: This object is a vintage wooden sewing machine model, featuring a rectangular wooden base supporting two upright posts. A horizontal wooden bar with gear teeth runs through the posts, engaging with a central gear mechanism. The gear assembly is exposed and crafted from wood, with visible cogs and a crank handle extending below the main body. The overall construction is simple, with all visible parts made from a light brown wood, and the gears and rack mechanism are clearly visible from the sides. The base is flat and sturdy, providing stability for the mechanical components above. There are no visible electrical components, branding, or modern features, giving it a distinctly historical and mechanical appearance.
description: A vintage wooden sewing machine model with exposed gears and a crank handle, mounted on a rectangular wooden base. All components are crafted from light brown wood, giving it a historical, mechanical look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full gear mechanism, crank handle, and both upright posts on the wooden base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile, with the gear teeth and crank handle barely visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the gear assembly and posts from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the thin profile and the length of the horizontal bar.
